methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Lumaril operates in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector at the Seed stage, having raised $12M to date. The valuation landscape includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers, with top-tier reference points including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Stage-Progression methodology is applicable here, using the $12M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression. This yields an implied valuation of $30M. The Peer-Median approach was not applicable; while seven same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, insufficient data points met the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables) to produce a reliable median. A broader sector median would risk obscuring meaningful distinctions in stage and scale.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $26M to $34M, with a median point of $30M. This reflects the Stage-Progression methodology applied in isolation. Key uncertainties include the absence of closely matched peer data at Seed stage within the robotics subsector, which limits triangulation rigor and warrants caution around point estimates. Validation through additional recent seed or early-stage financings in robotics, or clarification of Lumaril's technical differentiation relative to the peer set, would strengthen conviction in this range.
